seoTitle: How to Use Presenter View in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to use Presenter View in Canva to see your notes and upcoming slides while your audience only sees the main content.
intro: Presenter View lets you keep speaker notes and a preview of upcoming slides visible only to you, while your audience sees a clean, distraction-free presentation. This is especially useful for business meetings, lectures, and webinars. This guide covers turning it on and using it well.

steps.heading: Open your presentation design
steps.body: Go into the Canva presentation you plan to deliver.

steps.heading: Add speaker notes to your slides
steps.body: Use the notes section below each slide to write reminders or talking points.

steps.heading: Start Present mode
steps.body: Click Present, then choose the Presenter View option instead of the standard fullscreen mode.

steps.heading: Navigate using your notes and slide preview
steps.body: Use the split view showing your notes and the next slide to stay on track while presenting.

steps.heading: Share your screen correctly
steps.body: If presenting over a video call, share only the audience-facing window so your notes stay private.

faq.question: Can my audience see my speaker notes in Presenter View?
faq.answer: No, notes are only visible to you; the audience sees just the main presentation slide.

faq.question: Do I need Canva Pro to use Presenter View?
faq.answer: Presenter View is generally available to Canva users presenting from the platform, though check your plan for any feature limits.

faq.question: Can I use Presenter View on a video call?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you're careful to share only the presentation window and not your full screen.

faq.question: Can I edit my notes during a live presentation?
faq.answer: It's best to finalize your notes before presenting, since editing mid-presentation can be distracting.
